name: the-water-is-wide
description: Pat Conroy's The Water Is Wide — a memoir of teaching impoverished Black children on Yamacraw Island, challenging an unjust system, and the transformative power of education.
useCase: Teaching with impact, fighting an unjust system, crossing cultural divides, seeing potential others miss, choosing integrity, education as liberation.
knownRisks: Conroy was a white teacher in a Black community — the power dynamics are complex
The book was written in 1971 — some attitudes reflect the era
Conroy's methods were effective but controversial — adapt to your context
